provenance.source_corpus: Exponential fertilization promotes seedling growth by increasing nitrogen retranslocation in trembling aspen planted for oil sands reclamation

source_spans.text: Exponential fertilization promotes seedling growth by increasing nitrogen retranslocation in trembling aspen planted for oil sands reclamation
Exponential fertilization promotes seedling growth by increasing nitrogen retranslocation in trembling aspen planted for oil sands reclamation
Exponential fertilization promotes seedling growth by increasing nitrogen retranslocation in trembling aspen planted for oil sands reclamation
Exponential fertilization and weed removal increased height and root collar diameter growth but did not affect seedling survival over two growing seasons.
Weed competition reduced N retranslocation by 37% ( P < 0.01) and N uptake by seedlings from the soil by 61% ( P = 0.01) on the LFH site.
Exponential fertilization increased new stem and leaf biomass and N content but decreased the percent allocation of biomass to roots.

nodes.name: N uptake by seedlings from the soil

source_spans.text: Weed competition reduced N retranslocation by 37% ( P < 0.01) and N uptake by seedlings from the soil by 61% ( P = 0.01) on the LFH site.

nodes.name: Percent allocation of biomass to roots

source_spans.text: Exponential fertilization increased new stem and leaf biomass and N content but decreased the percent allocation of biomass to roots.
New stem biomass allocation was significantly increased by both exponential fertilization ( P < 0.01) and weed removal ( P < 0.01) on the PMM site
